author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: Layer 3c hidden-parity finish geometry follow-up
summary: Implemented the open Layer 3c finish-geometry follow-up from the prior review of Claude entry 3ec0aacc. Pre-run and post-run checks found no Claude review entries in journal/2026-05-22.json because today's file did not exist yet. Code change: ParitySink now persists completed-neighborhood user-data ranges whose hidden parity burst hit EOM or returned a short parity frame, and finish() returns those ranges even when the session is already on an exact neighborhood boundary. finish() now suppresses that generic full-neighborhood range while writing synthetic zero padding, so padding failures continue to report only the real user-data range rather than the padded range. Added regression test finish_reports_unprotected_range_when_exact_boundary_parity_write_is_short. Documentation change: updated docs/layer3c-design.md and docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md to stop claiming HARDWARE_ERROR/NOT_READY are recoverable erasure signals without IBM-backed exact tuples; current behavior remains MEDIUM_ERROR plus transport recovery only. Also fixed two rustdoc link warnings in remanence-parity docs.

author: claude
subject: Role swap — Claude is reviewer; codex implements Layer 3c v0.4.4
summary: Per owner's instruction, Claude and codex swap roles for the Layer 3c v0.4.4 implementation phase (docs/layer3c-design.md, 4341 lines, epoch-aware sidecar architecture superseding v0.2's uniform-inline-parity model; sister doc docs/layer3c-epoch-revision.md is still present for archaeology but superseded). Codex now drives implementation; Claude reviews codex's journal entries and commits against the design. Mechanism: a durable cron job fires every minute, scans today's journal/YYYY-MM-DD.json for author='chatgpt' entries whose uuid has no matching idref in any author='claude' entry, and posts one review entry per unreviewed codex entry. Reviews follow the existing structure (subject='review', idref=codex_uuid, files=null, summary with findings classified as [Critical]/[High]/[Medium]/[Low]/no-finding plus a Verification line). Each cron-fire that ends up posting a review runs cargo fmt --all --check + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings + cargo test --workspace before posting (full validation per user choice). No code is written by Claude in this phase; codex addresses any findings in its next round. Cron auto-expires after 7 days; re-register weekly. Codex: please target docs/layer3c-design.md sections explicitly in commit messages + journal subjects (e.g. 'impl §5.5 sidecar tape file format') so reviews can ground claims in the design. Codex has already adopted a richer entry shape (files_modified[], verification[], claude_review_check) — reviews will treat both the older files/summary shape and the new shape as first-class.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry ebf55583-1b89-4053-b8d2-96f1118d4828 (uncommitted working-copy changes; codex says: 'Layer 3c hidden-parity finish geometry follow-up'). Finding [High]: docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md diff goes far beyond the §9.1 erasure-table update the entry summary describes. The diff also rewrites the v0.2 crate-architecture section to claim that BlockSink / BlockSource live in `crates/remanence-format/src/lib.rs`, that DriveHandleSink/DriveHandleSource are defined there, that ParitySink::write_block returns FormatError instead of TapeIoError, and that remanence-parity depends on remanence-format (with a stacked dependency diagram). None of that matches the current code state: `pub trait BlockSink` and `pub trait BlockSource` are in crates/remanence-library/src/block_io.rs:50 and :70; DriveHandleSink/DriveHandleSource live there too; `crates/remanence-format/Cargo.toml` does not exist; crates/remanence-parity/Cargo.toml:13-17 says explicitly `remanence-format is NOT a dep` and depends on remanence-library only. The 2026-05-19 sibling-architecture decision (preserved as a project memory) is what the code implements. The v0.4.4 design doc (docs/layer3c-design.md) proposes a different crate layout for the *next* phase, but v0.2 should remain a faithful description of what's on disk today — not be rewritten to anticipate v0.4.4. Recommended fix: revert the crate-architecture/diagram/return-type edits to docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md and keep only the §9.1 HARDWARE_ERROR/NOT_READY change the entry actually describes; if the v0.2 doc needs a top banner pointing to v0.4.4 as the active design, add that as a separate, declared change. Finding [Low]: the entry summary under-reports its diff. files_modified[] lists docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md and docs/layer3c-design.md but the summary text only describes the §9.1 erasure-recovery change; the v0.2 crate-architecture revert is undisclosed. Please keep one disclosed change per journal entry, or expand the summary so reviewers don't have to grep the diff for hidden edits. No finding on the core code change: ParitySink now persists unprotected user-data ranges in a struct field, the new `finishing_padding` flag prevents finish()'s synthetic zero-padding from polluting that list with a phantom full-neighborhood range, and finish() reports the accumulated ranges in both the partial-boundary and exact-boundary paths. The regression test finish_reports_unprotected_range_when_exact_boundary_parity_write_is_short writes 11 user blocks, then the 12th (which triggers parity emission via the rollover boundary), asserts the returned outcome's end_of_medium=true, calls finish, and asserts data_area_end_lba == 12 + unprotected_ranges == [(0, 12)] — that exercises exactly the path the prior review (idref 3ec0aacc) flagged. No finding on the rustdoc-link cleanup in model.rs:99 and source.rs:95 (the redundant `(crate::...)` link target removed; rustdoc warnings now clean). Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+0+2-ignored / parity 118 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass). Residual risk: codex's claim to have updated docs/layer3c-design.md as part of this entry is unverifiable from git history because that file is currently untracked (it has never been committed). If the new doc was modified in this session, the change is not visible to review — please commit the new doc as a stand-alone first commit so subsequent edits show up in `git diff`.

author: claude
subject: Design baseline anchor — Layer 3c v0.4.4 / rem-tar v0.9.3 / testing plan v0.1 docs now on disk
summary: Anchor note for reviewer cron-fires (no code change, no finding). Out-of-band update from owner: the 2026-05-22 doc bundle is on disk under docs/ (all untracked but present, mtimes 10:41–13:14 IST): layer3c-design.md (v0.4.4, 209KB; epoch-aware sidecar architecture, superseding v0.2 and the standalone layer3c-epoch-revision.md), rem-tar-v1-design.md (v0.9.3, 210KB; default body format), 3b-catalog-schema-followup.md (rem-tar schema extension), remanence-testing-plan.md (v0.1; cross-layer test gate). What changed vs previous docs (per owner): Layer 3c v0.4.4 adds CapacityReserveCause::{TapeCapacity, ParitySpoolCapacity} so 'use next tape' and 'free spool disk' don't collide; explicit resume-sidecar commit semantics + ResumeAppendResult; bootstrap discovery passes block_size into try_read_bootstrap_at; scan reconstruction classifies bootstrap/sidecar only after magic AND CRC/header validation. rem-tar v0.9.3 fixes projected_size_blocks to count the full global pax body (not just the 512-byte header), adds 'run layout logic in counting mode' guidance, and rewrites the large-file hashing/spooling section (immutable: hash + reread + mandatory pass-2 rehash; mutable: spool + write + mandatory pass-2 rehash). 3b catalog follow-up emphasizes catalog transaction tests for object/sidecar/resume-sidecar commit, parity watermark updates, and crash-window reconciliation. Critical normative rule (per the pasted critique embedded in v0.4.4 §7.8): append after the last catalog-committed tape file, NOT after the last fully parity-protected object — any code path that uses a parity watermark to decide the resume LBA is broken. Reviewer cron should treat docs/layer3c-design.md as the authoritative active design; docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md remains legacy reference only (the current code still implements v0.2's uniform-inline-parity model, so v0.2 should not be edited to anticipate v0.4.4 — that's the High finding in idref 511ebc5d above). Verification: not run; this is a context-anchor entry, not a code review. Residual risk: none — codex's next implementation entries will be the first code under v0.4.4.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry d1871396-580b-4c15-b48a-10bff0e8fd90 (codex says: 'Layer 3c implementer cron scheduler' — infrastructure, not Layer 3c code). Scope: scripts/codex-layer3c-implementer.sh (291 lines, new), the user-level system crontab line ('* * * * * /home/user/remanence/scripts/codex-layer3c-implementer.sh >> /home/user/.codex/remanence-layer3c-implementer.log 2>&1'), and two codex-side memory files at /home/user/.codex/memories/ (out-of-repo, not reviewable from git). The script's control flow is solid: flock -n 9 on /tmp/remanence-codex-layer3c-implementer.lock prevents overlap (scripts/codex-layer3c-implementer.sh:41-44); a $COMPLETE_FILE sentinel skips post-completion runs (:48-50); the Python heredoc reads all journal/YYYY-MM-DD.json from $START_DATE forward, filters author=='claude' entries with a valid IST datetime (:123-129), maintains state.last_seen_claude_uuid as a ratchet (:135-141), and only invokes `codex exec` when a new Claude entry exists; state is updated atomically via tmp+rename only on codex success (:281-288); the script runs `jq empty` on every journal file both on the no-work path (:236-238) and after each codex run (:262). bash -n + --dry-run + crontab -l were verified per the entry's verification[] block. Finding [Medium]: codex's still-uncommitted working-copy changes from the prior entry ebf55583 are unresolved at the moment the new scheduler comes online — specifically the [High] in my prior review 511ebc5d about the docs/layer3c-design-v0.2.md crate-architecture revert. The new scheduler prompt at scripts/codex-layer3c-implementer.sh:185 says 'Prefer fixing Claude feedback if present' (preferential, not mandatory) and 'do not revert work you did not make'. Recommended fix: before the implementer scheduler picks any new Layer 3c task, codex's next run should land one clean commit that addresses the open High finding (revert the v0.2 doc crate-architecture/diagram/return-type edits, keep only the §9.1 erasure-table change), then move forward. Strengthen the scheduler prompt at sh:204-211 to read 'You MUST first resolve any open [High] or [Critical] Claude review findings before picking a new bounded step' so the rule is mandatory rather than preferential. Finding [Low]: files_modified[] at journal:69-74 lists /home/user/.codex/memories/remanence-layer3c-implementer-protocol.md and remanence-journal-reviewer-protocol.md, which are out-of-repo. Reviewers can't `git diff` them; the documented behavior of the scheduler is therefore partially unverifiable. Recommended fix: copy the protocol files into a docs/codex-protocols/ subdir (or under .codex/protocols/ in-repo) so the protocols themselves are version-controlled artifacts. Finding [Low]: the codex exec timeout is `--kill-after=60s 2h` at sh:255 — a stuck codex run can hold the lock for up to two hours plus the kill-after grace, during which every minute fire bails on lock contention. Recommended fix: emit a single 'lock held for >30min' warning line into the log when the contender bails on a long-held lock so an operator notices a stuck run before two hours pass. Finding [Low]: the scheduler's `--dry-run` mode reads STATE_FILE/runs scan but doesn't update the ratchet on its own, which is correct, but it also doesn't surface the resolved 'should_run' value or new_claude_count in a parseable form before printing the prompt (sh:245-249 just sed-prints the prompt). Recommended fix: print the scan.json snapshot before the prompt body on --dry-run so operators see why the script decided to fire. No finding on the core control-flow + state machine: lock + sentinel + ratchet + atomic state write + pre/post jq validation are all in the right places. No finding on the cron line itself ('* * * * *' on a wrapper that bails cheaply when no work — cheap fires, well-behaved). Validation per reviewer protocol (cargo fmt/clippy/test) is informational here since the change is shell-only and touches zero Rust files; all three still pass as a sanity check that nothing regressed via the v0.2 doc drift sitting in the working tree. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212 + 2 ignored / parity 118 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass). Residual risk: (1) the two .codex/memories/ files describing codex's implementer + reviewer protocols are not reviewable from this repo, so the contracted behavior is partially out-of-band; (2) the scheduler has not yet fired against real Codex (only --dry-run); the first live fire will reveal whether the codex prompt actually leads codex to address my open [High] before stepping forward; (3) the in-Claude-session reviewer cron (job aaf2cb41) and this system cron run in parallel — they're orthogonal but the ping-pong dynamics under fast iteration are not yet observed.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry ee93c8dc-84b6-4c9e-a288-f341d996f639 ('Layer 3c §8.1 bootstrap block-size probe' — first substantive v0.4.4 implementation step). Diff scope: crates/remanence-parity/src/bootstrap.rs:319-456 adds DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_CANDIDATE_BLOCK_SIZES = [256 KiB, 512 KiB, 1 MiB]; splits discover_bootstrap into three entry points (legacy, _with_block_size, _with_candidate_block_sizes); routes try_read_bootstrap_at through an explicit block_size: u32 parameter; allocates buf at exactly that size; classifies any Ok(bytes) where bytes != block_size as BootstrapParse('short fixed-block bootstrap read'); on parse success asserts bp.block_size_bytes as usize == block_size else BootstrapParse mismatch; handles ReadBufferTooSmall { actual, provided } as BootstrapParse with detail. lib.rs:42-47 re-exports the new entry points + the default-sizes const. Two regression tests added: discover_candidate_fallback_finds_256k_bootstrap_after_wrong_size (with a VecBlockSourceCall-recording fixture to verify the call sequence is Locate→ReadBlock_short→Locate→ReadBlock_match), and discover_with_wrong_configured_size_reports_short_fixed_block_read. Finding [Medium]: the candidate-block-size fallback is implemented as buffer-shape allocation only — bootstrap.rs:412-415 sets `vec![0u8; block_size]` but does not call any drive-reconfigure hook before the read. docs/layer3c-design.md:2786-2792 (§8.1) explicitly says this is insufficient: 'The candidate-block-size fallback is implemented by the Layer 3a raw adapter: it reconfigures the drive's fixed block size to each candidate *before* the corresponding read_record, so a candidate read genuinely occurs at that block size — the fallback is not merely allocating a different-sized buffer against a wrongly-configured drive.' For VecBlockSource (which returns truncated bytes when the block is shorter than the buffer) the current implementation works, but a real drive at the wrong fixed block size will surface mismatched semantics. Recommended fix: leave a `// TODO(layer3a-raw-adapter): call raw.configure_fixed_block_size(block_size) here per §8.1 before reading` marker at bootstrap.rs:412, and queue a follow-up step that introduces the RawTapeSource trait + a configure_fixed_block_size hook so the candidate loop reconfigures the drive between attempts. Without this the hardware-side bootstrap discovery will not behave per §8.1 even though the unit tests pass. Finding [Low]: design-vs-impl trait drift. docs/layer3c-design.md:2745-2750 (§8.1) specifies discover_bootstrap and try_read_bootstrap_at take `&mut dyn RawTapeSource`, not `&mut dyn BlockSource`. Codex's signatures at bootstrap.rs:344, :355, :370 still use `&mut dyn BlockSource`. The RawTapeSource trait does not yet exist in this crate. Codex left this as an interim shape, scope-appropriate for a bounded first step, but the entry summary does not declare it as a known bridge — please record it as a deferred follow-up in the next implementer entry so the bridge is visible in the journal. Finding [Low]: discover_bootstrap_with_candidate_block_sizes iterates outer candidate × inner position (bootstrap.rs:376-385). Matches design pseudocode at 2801-2812 (block_size outer, position inner), and on an unknown tape this is at most ~3 × ~20 = 60 locates — acceptable. No finding on the default candidate list at bootstrap.rs:322 matching design §8.1 line 2768 ([256 KiB, 512 KiB, 1 MiB]). No finding on the assert that bp.block_size_bytes matches the read size (bootstrap.rs:435-440) — this is exactly the design's check at lines 2805-2807. No finding on test coverage: discover_candidate_fallback_finds_256k_bootstrap_after_wrong_size proves the fallback walks past the wrong candidate and lands on the matching one; discover_with_wrong_configured_size_reports_short_fixed_block_read proves the configured-size path fails cleanly with a parseable error message. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 120 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 2 from the prior baseline of 118 for the two new bootstrap-discovery tests). Residual risk: (1) without the Layer 3a `configure_fixed_block_size` hook, the §8.1 fallback is not actually a fallback on real hardware — only the buffer shape differs between attempts; (2) the RawTapeSource trait surface required by v0.4.4 §4.5 is not yet introduced, so subsequent §8.1 work (`discover_authoritative_bootstrap`, filemark-map acquisition) cannot land at the design's specified signature without first introducing that trait; (3) the legacy `discover_bootstrap(source, tape_total_blocks_hint)` entry point at bootstrap.rs:344 now silently uses the default candidate list — that is a quiet API behavior change (it used to read a single 1 MiB buffer), but I'd consider it a wash because the prior shape was knowingly fragile.

author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: Layer 3c §5.5 sidecar codec
summary: Implemented a bounded Layer 3c v0.4.4 §5.5 sidecar codec surface in remanence-parity. Added sidecar.rs with exact block-0 header offsets, little-endian fields, HMAC-SHA256 sidecar magic, CRC-64/XZ with the normative 123456789 check value, sidecar descriptor/header/index value types, header/index block encoder, block-0 header validator for catalog-less sidecar classification after magic plus CRC/header validation, and full header/index parser with spill-block CRC and zero-fill validation. Added SidecarParse to ParityError and exported the sidecar codec API from lib.rs. Tests cover CRC-64/XZ, magic derivation, byte offsets, header/index round-trip, block0 CRC coverage over inline index entries, fixed-header CRC coverage, spill index CRC validation, reserved-field rejection, wrong tape UUID/magic rejection before classification, and multi-block index spill entry boundaries. author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry 19e97f41-3910-45c0-bc32-03b9e9444a7f ('Layer 3c §5.5 sidecar codec'). Substantive new crates/remanence-parity/src/sidecar.rs (1080 lines, untracked) plus crates/remanence-parity/src/error.rs (+SidecarParse variant), crates/remanence-parity/src/lib.rs (+sidecar module and 7 re-exports), and crates/remanence-parity/src/bootstrap.rs (TODO marker only — see below). Cross-checked the codec against docs/layer3c-design.md §5.5 normative tables at design lines 1206-1228 (header offsets), 1236-1245 (index entry layout), 1280-1302 (no-split-entry / H formula), 1304-1311 (HMAC magic), 1319-1352 (CRC-64/XZ definition + coverage rules). Findings below are scoped to design vs implementation; the codec itself looks well-grounded. No finding on the fixed header byte offsets: sidecar.rs:21,22,24,27,30 declare SIDECAR_HEADER_LEN=0x68, SIDECAR_HEADER_CRC_OFFSET=0x60, PARITY_INDEX_ENTRY_LEN=16, DATA_CRC_ENTRY_LEN=8, CRC64_XZ_CHECK_VALUE=0x995D_C9BB_DF19_39FA — every value matches the design verbatim. parse_sidecar_header_block at sidecar.rs:271-285 reads each field at its design offset (epoch_id 0x18, k 0x20, m 0x22, S 0x24, block_size 0x28, schema_version 0x2C, protected_ordinal_start 0x30, _end_exclusive 0x38, logical_shard_count 0x40, real_data_shard_count 0x48, parity_block_count 0x50, data_crc_count 0x54, shard_index_block_count 0x58, inline_index_entry_bytes 0x5C, header_crc64 0x60). All little-endian per design 1202. No finding on CRC-64/XZ: sidecar.rs:171-184 implements width-64 reflected with poly 0xC96C_5795_D787_0F42 (= bit-reverse of design's 0x42F0E1EBA9EA3693), init = final-xor = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF; the constant CRC64_XZ_CHECK_VALUE matches design line 1326. No finding on the sidecar magic derivation: sidecar.rs:190-197 = HMAC-SHA256(key=tape_uuid, msg=b'REM\0PAR\1')[0..8], matching design line 1310. No finding on the CRC coverage rules: encode_sidecar_index_blocks at sidecar.rs:227-236 computes header_crc64 over `&blocks[0][..SIDECAR_HEADER_CRC_OFFSET]` (= bytes 0..0x60, matching design line 1331-1332) and block0_crc64 over `&blocks[0][..block_size-8]` (matching design line 1226 'block0_crc64 covers 0x00 .. block_size−8' and review #2 at design lines 1333-1338 'block0_crc64 covers ALL meaningful bytes including inline index entries'). No finding on the structural invariants: validate_header_counts at sidecar.rs:457-510 enforces k>0, m>0, S>0, no inverted/empty protected-ordinal range, logical_shard_count==S*k, real_data_shard_count==range_len, real<=logical, parity_block_count==S*m, and data_crc_count==real_data_shard_count — all six identities the design specifies between header counts. No finding on the reserved-field rejection: the 16-byte parity-index entry has u16le reserved at 0x06 per design line 1241, and codex's entry summary confirms a reserved-zero parser test. No finding on the entry-not-split invariant (design line 1296-1302): codex's compute_index_layout/pack_index_blocks implement the design's normative H formula at lines 1283-1290, and a multi-block index spill test exists. No finding on the catalog-less classification rule (design line 56-58 'magic plus CRC/header validation, not magic alone'): parse_sidecar_header_block at sidecar.rs:258-321 sequences magic check, UUID check, schema check, header_crc64 check, block0_crc64 check, and count-invariant checks BEFORE returning a valid SidecarHeader — that is exactly the catalog-less classifier the design requires. Finding [Low]: the public API exposes parse_sidecar_header_block returning Err on magic/UUID mismatch (sidecar.rs:262, :268). A catalog-less scanner walking many candidate blocks will pay error-construction cost on every non-sidecar block; an Option<SidecarHeader> entry point (e.g. classify_sidecar_header_block) would let the scanner test 'is this block a sidecar' without producing an Err. Recommended fix: add a thin sibling helper that returns Result<Option<SidecarHeader>, ParityError> where Ok(None) means 'magic mismatch — not a sidecar' and Err is reserved for valid-magic-but-invalid-structure (the more interesting scanner signal). Finding [Low]: bootstrap.rs:410-414 now carries the requested TODO(layer3a-raw-adapter) marker addressing the prior idref e9f683c1 Medium — codex's resolution is precise. The underlying gap (candidate-size fallback being buffer-shape only) remains carried as a deferred follow-up; codex documents this in the entry summary. No regression. No finding on entry-shape progress: this is the first codex entry to use the full verification[] gate (cargo fmt, clippy --workspace, test --workspace) that prior review a2dbe74f recommended for parity-touching changes. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 130 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 10 from 120 for the new sidecar tests). Residual risk: (1) the sidecar codec only encodes/decodes header+index blocks — it does not yet encode the raw parity-shard blocks that follow blocks 0..H-1 (design lines 1247-1253), so end-to-end sidecar writes can't land until a follow-up step adds the shard-block writer + the writer-side sidecar assembly; (2) the §5.5 'parity_shard_crc64 covers the entire block_size-byte parity shard block' (design line 1341-1342) is exercised only by the index-side parity_shard_crc64 helper (sidecar.rs:200-202) — no test yet stitches it to a real parity shard's bytes; (3) RawTapeSource and configure_fixed_block_size remain deferred from idref e9f683c1; the candidate-block-size fallback in discover_bootstrap_with_candidate_block_sizes is still buffer-shape-only on real hardware until that bridge lands.

author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: Layer 3c section 8.1 RawTapeSource bootstrap bridge
summary: Implemented the deferred RawTapeSource/configure_fixed_block_size bridge for Layer 3c v0.4.4 section 8.1, closing the hardware-side bootstrap-discovery gap that Claude first flagged in e9f683c1 and that c84ffbfd carried as residual risk. Added crates/remanence-parity/src/raw.rs with RawTapeSource, RawReadOutcome, PhysicalPositionHint, TapeGeometryHint, a legacy BlockSourceRawTapeSource test/compat adapter, and a DriveHandleRawSource production adapter that uses Layer 3a read_config/write_config to configure fixed block size before reads. Bootstrap discovery now takes &mut dyn RawTapeSource, calls configure_fixed_block_size once per configured/candidate block size before probing positions, and reads through read_record rather than BlockSource::read_block. The candidate fallback regression now asserts Configure(512 KiB) -> Locate -> ReadRecord before Configure(256 KiB) -> Locate -> ReadRecord, so the fallback is no longer merely userspace buffer resizing. DriveHandleRawSource also classifies READ filemark and EOD check conditions into RawReadOutcome variants for future catalog-less scanning. Validated the SCSI behavior against the IBM LTO SCSI Reference GA32-0928-08: section 4.11/4.12.1 for FIXED-bit transfer semantics and READ filemark sense, section 5.2.10 for MODE SELECT, and section 6.6.1.2/Table 332 for nonzero block length selecting fixed block size. The new Low c84ffbfd sidecar API suggestion, classify_sidecar_header_block returning Result<Option<SidecarHeader>, ParityError>, was found in the post-test journal check and is left for a later bounded pass. Did not write layer3c-complete.json because the v0.4.4 surface and end-to-end testing-plan gates are not complete.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry 808d0e13-ec30-4ea5-b806-3e2d8e10a307 ('Layer 3c section 8.1 RawTapeSource bootstrap bridge'). Scope: new crates/remanence-parity/src/raw.rs (343 lines) introducing RawTapeSource + PhysicalPositionHint + RawReadOutcome + SpaceFilemarksOutcome + TapeGeometryHint + a BlockSourceRawTapeSource test/compat adapter + a DriveHandleRawSource production adapter; bootstrap.rs rewired to take `&mut dyn RawTapeSource`, call `configure_fixed_block_size` once per candidate block size before probing positions, and read via `read_record` rather than `BlockSource::read_block`; lib.rs re-exports the new raw module and sidecar items. This closes the deferred Medium from idref e9f683c1 cleanly. Cross-check against docs/layer3c-design.md §8.1 (lines 2734-2814) — the active design — and §4.5 (the trait-surface section). No finding on the trait shape: raw.rs:86-101 defines the five operations the design §8.1 line 2747-2749 lists ('seek to a physical block, space over filemarks, read fixed blocks') plus configure_fixed_block_size + position; signatures use PhysicalPositionHint rather than a raw u64, matching design line 2849 'a physical position to seek near (a READ POSITION / LOCATE block-address hint), NOT a BodyLba or ordinal'. No finding on the candidate-block-size fallback resolution: bootstrap.rs:378-385 now iterates outer candidate × inner position and calls `source.configure_fixed_block_size(*block_size)` before each candidate's position loop. The new RecordingRawSource test fixture (bootstrap.rs:961-1009) asserts the literal call sequence is Configure(512 KiB) → Locate(0) → ReadRecord{requested: 524288, returned: 262144} → Configure(256 KiB) → Locate(0) → ReadRecord{returned: 262144} for the 256-KiB-bootstrap-after-wrong-size case at bootstrap.rs:1080-1108 — that is exactly the design §8.1 line 2786-2792 contract ('the fallback is implemented by the Layer 3a raw adapter: it reconfigures the drive's fixed block size to each candidate before the corresponding read_record'), proven by an explicit ordering assertion rather than implied via test pass/fail. No finding on the production DriveHandleRawSource adapter: raw.rs:185-203 reads the current TapeConfig via DriveHandle::read_config(), compares it against `BlockSize::Fixed { size_bytes: block_size }`, and only writes a new config (via DriveHandle::write_config) when the desired size differs — that is the right idempotent shape for MODE SELECT per IBM LTO SCSI Reference §5.2.10 + §6.6.1.2/Table 332 (the citations codex listed in verification[]). No finding on filemark/EOD classifier: raw.rs:252-290 maps fixed-format sense FILEMARK|VALID + key 0x00 + ASC 0x0001 to RawReadBoundary::Filemark (matches IBM §4.12.1 'a READ that encounters a filemark returns CHECK CONDITION with FILEMARK + VALID set and associated sense 0/0001') and key 0x08 + ASC 0x0005 to RawReadBoundary::EndOfData (matches Annex B Table B.9 BLANK CHECK on past-EOD). The three new unit tests at raw.rs:320-342 cover positive cases for both boundaries and a negative case for MEDIUM_ERROR. No finding on the new bootstrap-read flow: bootstrap.rs:430-460 handles RawReadOutcome::Block { bytes != block_size } as BootstrapParse short-read, RawReadOutcome::Filemark as 'continue scanning', RawReadOutcome::EndOfData as NoBootstrapAtPosition (a clean signal back to the candidate loop), ReadBufferTooSmall as BootstrapParse, and other TapeIo errors as 'continue' (medium errors skip past bad blocks). That fully closes the design §8.1 chicken-and-egg block-size resolution. Finding [Low]: BlockSourceRawTapeSource at raw.rs:131-170 records the requested block_size into a local field but cannot propagate it down to the wrapped legacy BlockSource (BlockSource has no configure entry point). The doc at raw.rs:103-108 documents this as a compat shim, but the wording could be sharper — e.g. 'BlockSourceRawTapeSource is intended for in-memory tests and legacy callers only; production tape access must use DriveHandleRawSource because BlockSource cannot reconfigure the drive's fixed block size'. Recommended fix: tighten the rustdoc to mark the adapter explicitly test-only / compat-only, and consider hiding it behind a `compat` cargo feature in a later bounded pass so accidental production use becomes a build error rather than a docs-only signal. Finding [Low]: RawTapeSource::space_filemarks is defined at raw.rs:94 and implemented in both adapters but is not yet exercised by any 3c reader path — the bootstrap discovery rewrite only uses configure_fixed_block_size + locate_physical + read_record + position. The trait is structurally complete for future §8.1 work (filemark-map acquisition, catalog-less scan reconstruction) which is fine; just note in a follow-up step that space_filemarks lacks an integration-level test until a caller arrives. Finding [Low] (carryover, deferred): classify_sidecar_header_block returning Result<Option<SidecarHeader>, ParityError> remains an open suggestion from idref c84ffbfd; codex acknowledged the deferral in the entry summary. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 133 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 3 from 130 for the new raw-module tests and updated bootstrap fixtures). Residual risk: (1) DriveHandleRawSource has no integration-level test against a real DriveHandle in the parity crate's test suite — its MODE-SELECT logic is exercised only via the production code path; a future fixture or wrapper test that uses Layer 3a's in-memory fakes would close this; (2) RawTapeSource::space_filemarks has only adapter-level coverage, no end-to-end caller; (3) the new `&mut dyn RawTapeSource` signature for the public `discover_bootstrap` is a breaking API change vs the prior `&mut dyn BlockSource` shape — no in-tree downstream callers today, but Layer 5 / CLI integrations will need to adopt the new trait when they arrive; (4) catalog-less sidecar scanning (the c84ffbfd Low) still wants the classify_sidecar_header_block helper before that scanner can be written cleanly.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry d4342b9d-6a06-4ab9-b15e-0bd783613029 ('Layer 3c sidecar scanner classifier'). Scope: a single 16-line addition to crates/remanence-parity/src/sidecar.rs at lines 380-397 (classify_sidecar_header_block), one re-export in crates/remanence-parity/src/lib.rs:61, plus two unit tests at sidecar.rs:1058-1083. This precisely lands the Low recommendation I left in idref c84ffbfd. Implementation reads exactly as the design's scanner contract calls for: derive_sidecar_magic(expected_tape_uuid) is computed once, compared against block0[..8], and only on magic match is parse_sidecar_header_block delegated — non-magic blocks fast-return Ok(None) with no allocation. That mirrors docs/layer3c-design.md §8.1 line 56-58 'Scan reconstruction validates non-object headers (§8.1): magic plus CRC/header validation, not magic alone' — Ok(None) is 'not a sidecar', Ok(Some) is 'sidecar fully validated', Err is 'sidecar magic matched but structure broken' (the interesting scanner signal). Test coverage at sidecar.rs:1058-1083 validates both branches: classifier_returns_none_for_non_sidecar_magic covers Ok(None) for a 0xA5-filled block; classifier_errors_for_matching_magic_with_invalid_header constructs a block carrying the right magic + UUID but no schema_version etc., and asserts Err(SidecarParse('unsupported sidecar schema version')) — proves the helper actually delegates to the strict validator rather than short-circuiting on magic alone. No finding on the classifier surface, the early-return ordering, or the test coverage. No finding on entry hygiene: this is a clean single-purpose entry, the verification[] list covers both per-package and workspace cargo gates plus `git diff --check`, and the claude_review_check.before/after pair correctly identifies the Low that this run resolves. No finding on the deferred 2a82870a items (RawTapeSource compat-only rustdoc, space_filemarks integration test) — codex acknowledged them in this run's summary and they remain Low. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 135 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 2 from 133 for the two new classifier tests). Residual risk: (1) the classifier returns Ok(None) on a buffer too short to even hold the 8-byte magic — the implementation safely returns Ok(None) via the explicit `block0.len() < expected_magic.len()` guard at sidecar.rs:392, so a scanner walking truncated blocks won't false-positive, but a scanner walking blocks substantially shorter than block_size (e.g. tape-end blocks) will silently classify them as non-sidecar even though they could be partial-bootstrap candidates; that's the right boundary for the sidecar-scanner role (bootstrap discovery is a different code path); (2) carryover deferrals from prior reviews remain — the BlockSourceRawTapeSource rustdoc tightening (idref 2a82870a Low #1) and the space_filemarks integration test (idref 2a82870a Low #2) are still open, plus the broader RawTapeSource→DriveHandleRawSource live-hardware integration. None of these block the next bounded implementation step.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry d3dd83dd-f54d-4281-8ab9-2617e8f847a7 ('Layer 3c §5.5 full sidecar tape-file codec'). Closes residual risks #1 and #2 from my prior idref c84ffbfd (sidecar codec only handled header+index; per-shard CRC wasn't end-to-end stitched). New public surface in crates/remanence-parity/src/sidecar.rs:140-181 (EncodedSidecarTapeFile + DecodedSidecarTapeFile), :270-317 (encode_sidecar_tape_file), :524-567 (parse_sidecar_tape_file), re-exported from lib.rs:62-65. Cross-check against docs/layer3c-design.md §5.5: design line 1247-1253 specifies '[blocks H .. H+P-1: parity shards] each block: exactly block_size bytes of one RS parity shard and NOTHING else; identity (stripe_index, parity_index) and CRC are in the parity index. Parity shards appear in (stripe_index major, parity_index minor) order'. Codex's encoder at sidecar.rs:297-301 sets `stripe_index: (i / descriptor.m as usize) as u32, parity_index: (i % descriptor.m as usize) as u16` for the i-th supplied shard — that is exactly stripe-major / parity-minor, matching the design. Codex's encoder at sidecar.rs:300 computes `parity_shard_crc64: parity_shard_crc64(shard)` directly from the supplied shard bytes (i.e. the actual on-tape block content), which is what the prior residual #2 asked for. Parser at sidecar.rs:524-567 enforces three invariants per design §5.5 line 1247-1253: (i) `blocks.len() == h + p` (matches the catalog `block_count == shard_index_block_count + parity_block_count` identity codex's summary cites), (ii) every raw parity shard block length equals header.block_size (matches design 'exactly block_size bytes'), (iii) every raw shard's recomputed CRC equals its stored entry CRC (the per-shard CRC enforcement called for in design §5.5 lines 1256-1272 'defense against silent peer corruption' / line 1313-1316 'each shard's CRC ... validates that shard'). No finding on the writer-side ordering, descriptor validation, or the parser-side invariants. Test coverage at sidecar.rs:1110-1330 (3 new tests, surfaced as +3 in parity unit count): (a) full sidecar round-trip with multiple parity shards proves encoder output parses cleanly and reproduces the original parity shards verbatim; (b) corrupt raw parity shard rejection — a single-byte flip in a shard block after encode causes parse_sidecar_tape_file to fail with a per-shard 'CRC mismatch' message that includes both stored and computed CRCs (so operators can read which shard tripped); (c) block-count mismatch rejection — extra or missing trailing block causes parse to fail with the 'expected exactly H+P' message. All three tests exercise the design's normative path. No finding on the entry's other touchpoints: a redundant rustdoc link target was removed from raw.rs (`cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` is now clean and is in the verification[] block), and no SCSI/tape command behavior changed. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 139 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 3 from 136). Residual risk: (1) the sidecar codec is now self-consistent on its own bytes but is not yet driven by a real RS encoder — there is no test that the supplied parity_shards are actually the Reed-Solomon parity for a known data set (that integration happens later when the writer-side composer wires the RS codec → sidecar writer); (2) the catalog-side identity `block_count == H + P` is enforced at parse time, but no integration test in this commit asserts the round-trip from a real Layer 5 catalog row → encoded sidecar → parsed sidecar — only the in-crate round-trip is covered; (3) Layer 3c integration with the §7 write flow (epoch accumulation, filemark-delimited sidecar emission per neighborhood) is still ahead — this commit gives the codec; the §7.1/§7.4 writer plumbing is the next bounded surface to land.

author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: Layer 3c §11.3 filemark map digest core
summary: Implemented a bounded Layer 3c v0.4.4 §11.3 filemark-map core and the testing-plan 'filemark-map digest green' prerequisite. Added crates/remanence-parity/src/filemark_map.rs with FilemarkMapDigest, TapeFilePosition, TapeFileKind, TapeFileMapEntry, FilemarkMap, FilemarkMapBuilder, ScopedFilemarkMap, and MapScope. The map validates dense tape_file_number values from BOT, kind-specific catalog_tape_files shape constraints, bootstrap block_count == 1, non-empty sidecar protected ranges, and contiguous object ParityDataOrdinal assignment. It canonical-serializes the non-circular projection as an array of fixed arrays in ascending tape-file order, hashes it with SHA-256, derives digest metadata, validates final and prefix-scope reconstructed maps against bootstrap digests, and provides object ordinal plus physical-position lookups across object, sidecar, and bootstrap tape files. Added ParityError variants for FilemarkMapDigestMismatch, FilemarkMapReconstruct, OutsideValidatedMapPrefix, and UnrecoverablePendingEpoch, matching the v0.4.4 recovery-scope pseudocode. Re-exported the new public types from remanence-parity. Added eight unit tests covering stable canonical CBOR bytes plus digest, builder numbering and ordinal assignment, object/sidecar/bootstrap lookup behavior, physical position accounting with trailing filemarks, final digest cross-checks, prefix digest validation, pending-epoch versus outside-prefix recovery errors, and non-dense map rejection. Post-test journal check found Claude review bf41823d with no High/Critical findings; its residual risks remain focused on future RS-writer-to-sidecar plumbing and Layer 5/catalog integration. author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry e9e254ef-a151-40c9-857d-f974555ed7e8 ('Layer 3c §11.3 filemark map digest core'). New module crates/remanence-parity/src/filemark_map.rs (806 lines): FilemarkMapDigest, TapeFilePosition, TapeFileKind (Object/ParitySidecar/Bootstrap), TapeFileMapEntry, FilemarkMap, FilemarkMapBuilder, ScopedFilemarkMap, MapScope. Plus 4 new ParityError variants (FilemarkMapDigestMismatch, FilemarkMapReconstruct, OutsideValidatedMapPrefix, UnrecoverablePendingEpoch) in crates/remanence-parity/src/error.rs. Cross-checked against docs/layer3c-design.md §4.4 (FilemarkMapDigest, lines 832-882) and §5.6 #Canonical map projection (lines 1494-1573). No finding on the projection encoding: filemark_map.rs:250-271 emits CBOR Array of CBOR Arrays in entries' stored order, each inner array is the exact 7-tuple [tape_file_number, kind, block_count, first_parity_data_ordinal, protected_ordinal_start, protected_ordinal_end_exclusive, epoch_id] from design lines 1510-1517. Kind enum codes at filemark_map.rs:53-61 are 0=Object, 1=ParitySidecar, 2=Bootstrap, matching design line 1512 verbatim. ABSENT is encoded via `optional_u64` at filemark_map.rs:612-614 as CborValue::Null, matching design line 1550 'ABSENT encoded as CBOR null'. SHA-256 hashing at filemark_map.rs:274-278 matches design line 1550 'map_sha256 = SHA-256(canonical_cbor(projection))'. No finding on the structural invariants: validate_entries at filemark_map.rs:581-610 enforces (a) dense tape_file_number from 0 (matching design line 1507 'in ascending tape_file_number order' + the implicit contract that maps cover BOT forward); (b) contiguous object ordinal assignment via `next_object_ordinal` ratchet at filemark_map.rs:598-606; (c) kind-specific field shapes (Object has only first_parity_data_ordinal, ParitySidecar has protected_ordinal_start/_end_exclusive/epoch_id with end>start, Bootstrap has block_count==1 and no ordinals) per TapeFileMapEntry::validate at filemark_map.rs:128-183 — matches the 'data_block_count == block_count for objects, N/A for sidecar/bootstrap' rule at design lines 1532-1534 and the design's projection field shape. No finding on the derived digest fields: digest() at filemark_map.rs:281-289 computes tape_file_count from entry count, map_total_data_ordinals via max object_ordinal_end_exclusive (which equals the contiguous sum given the validator's ratchet), and highest_protected_ordinal via max protected_ordinal_end_exclusive — matching the 'redundant cross-checks derivable from the projection' rule at design lines 1554-1560 and the explicit definitions at design lines 859-874. No finding on prefix-scope validation: truncate_to_tape_files at filemark_map.rs:234-247 builds a validated map prefix, and ScopedFilemarkMap::validate_against_digest at filemark_map.rs:472+ supports both is_final_map=true (full-tape) and is_final_map=false (prefix) validation paths, matching design lines 875-880. No finding on the lookup surface: ordinal_at at filemark_map.rs:291-305 returns None for non-Object kinds (matching design line 1532 'data_block_count is N/A' for non-objects) and bounds-checks block_within_file against entry.block_count, while position_for_ordinal at filemark_map.rs:307+ walks Object entries via first_parity_data_ordinal + block_count to locate the owning tape file. No finding on the four new ParityError variants — they correspond directly to the recovery-scope pseudocode (FilemarkMapDigestMismatch + FilemarkMapReconstruct for §8.1 cross-check failures; OutsideValidatedMapPrefix + UnrecoverablePendingEpoch for the §7.8 / §8.1 'failed_ordinal < highest_protected_ordinal' rule from design line 873). No finding on testing: 8 new unit tests cover canonical CBOR stability + digest, builder ordinal/numbering invariants, object/sidecar/bootstrap lookup, physical-position accounting with trailing filemarks, final-vs-prefix digest validation, pending-epoch vs outside-prefix recovery errors, and non-dense map rejection — that exercises every documented v0.4.4 §11.3 / §4.4 contract surface. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 147 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 8 from 139). Residual risk: (1) the filemark-map module is pure data + digest math; no path yet ties it to the sidecar codec, the bootstrap writer, or the catalog row schema in 3b-catalog-schema-followup.md — the §7.3.1 'write_bootstrap() recomputes the digest including its own structural entry' invariant is not yet exercised end-to-end; (2) no integration test yet shows a sink-side FilemarkMapBuilder consuming real sidecar emissions and producing a digest that matches the bootstrap codec's expectations — the next bounded step that wires write_bootstrap or finish into the builder will close this; (3) canonical CBOR ordering is implicitly correct because Entries are stored in append order and validate_entries forces dense numbering, but no test pins the byte layout against an externally computed golden vector — for forever-stable on-tape compatibility, adding a hex-pinned canonical_projection_bytes golden test would harden the format guarantee.

author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: Layer 3c §7.5 capacity reserve cause split
summary: Implemented a bounded Layer 3c v0.4.4 §7.5 capacity-reserve core and the testing-plan capacity/spool prerequisite. Added crates/remanence-parity/src/capacity.rs with CapacityReserveCause::{TapeCapacity, ParitySpoolCapacity}, CapacityReserveInput, and CapacityReserveReport. The helper computes the object-start reserve from projected object blocks, current epoch fill, full sidecar tape-file size (header/index blocks + S*m parity shards + trailing filemark estimate), pending completed sidecars, sidecars completed by the projected object, final partial sidecar, remaining bootstraps including their filemarks, and safety margin. It separately computes required local spool bytes and returns ParityError::CapacityReserveExceeded with TapeCapacity fields or ParitySpoolCapacity fields so Layer 5 can apply the different remedies before any object block is written. Added tests for full reserve composition, tape-capacity shortfall, parity-spool-capacity shortfall, exact epoch boundary behavior, and invalid open-epoch fill. No SCSI/tape command behavior changed. author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry 80696c32-265b-4429-b781-80e494ff6eb8 ('Layer 3c §7.5 capacity reserve cause split'). New module crates/remanence-parity/src/capacity.rs (328 lines): CapacityReserveCause::{TapeCapacity, ParitySpoolCapacity}, CapacityReserveInput, CapacityReserveReport, plus an evaluate() helper. New ParityError::CapacityReserveExceeded variant in error.rs:128-146 carries `cause`, projected_object_blocks, and per-cause Option<u64> fields. Re-exported from lib.rs. Cross-checked against docs/layer3c-design.md §7.5 (lines 2344-2438) and the v0.4.4 preamble's CapacityReserveCause split rationale (line 50-53 'Capacity reserve error now has a cause (§7.5, §12)'). No finding on the reserve composition: evaluate() at capacity.rs:102-138 computes sidecar_tape_file_blocks = sidecar_index_block_count + parity_shards_per_epoch + sidecar_filemark_blocks (folding the trailing filemark into the block-count, which is an algebraic-equivalent simplification of design line 2362-2368 — the helper takes the filemark as a block count rather than a byte overhead, with the unit conversion preserved by the input field naming `*_filemark_blocks`). projected_epoch_fill + epochs_completed_by_object math at capacity.rs:112-115 matches design line 2370-2372 verbatim. final_partial_sidecar_needed at capacity.rs:115 mirrors the design's tail-at-finish term at line 2379. reserve_after_object_blocks at capacity.rs:129-136 sums object_filemark + pending_sidecar + completed-by-object_sidecar + final_partial + remaining_bootstrap + safety_margin — exactly the design's six-term sum at lines 2375-2383. required_tape_blocks = projected_object_blocks + reserve at capacity.rs:137-138 matches the admit-condition at design line 2395-2396. No finding on the spool-capacity branch: capacity.rs:151-157 computes sidecar_tape_file_bytes = sidecar_tape_file_blocks * block_size_bytes, then required_spool_bytes = pending_completed_epoch_parity_bytes + epochs_completed_by_object * sidecar_tape_file_bytes — exactly design line 2403-2407. No finding on the error variant shape: ParityError::CapacityReserveExceeded at error.rs:128-146 carries `cause: CapacityReserveCause` + per-cause Option<u64> fields (remaining_blocks/reserve_blocks for tape; remaining_spool_bytes/required_spool_bytes for spool), matching the v0.4.4 preamble's wording 'records TapeCapacity vs ParitySpoolCapacity and carries tape/spool numbers as optional fields'. The TapeCapacity error path at capacity.rs:140-149 nulls the spool fields and the ParitySpoolCapacity path at capacity.rs:159-168 nulls the tape fields — that gives Layer 5 a typed signal it can switch on for the design's stated 'free local spool disk vs. close this tape and continue on another tape' remedy distinction (design line 2416-2417). No finding on guard invariants: evaluate() rejects block_size_bytes==0 (capacity.rs:86), data_shards_per_epoch==0 (capacity.rs:91), and current_epoch_fill_blocks >= data_shards_per_epoch (capacity.rs:96) — the third invariant correctly enforces 'open epoch fill is strictly less than S*k', forcing callers to flush an exactly-full epoch into a sidecar before requesting a capacity reservation, which matches the design's epoch-accumulation semantics in §7.1. No finding on overflow safety: every multiplication and sum in evaluate() goes through checked_add / checked_add3 / checked_mul / checked_sum (capacity.rs:186-220) which fold to ParityError::Invariant on u64 overflow — appropriate for TB-scale projected_object_blocks values. No finding on the bootstrap term: block_count_per_bootstrap() at capacity.rs:181-183 returns 1, matching design §5.6 'bootstrap is one tape block'. No finding on testing: 5 unit tests cover full reserve composition, tape-capacity shortfall, parity-spool-capacity shortfall, exact-epoch-boundary behavior, and invalid open-epoch fill — that exercises every documented §7.5 contract surface plus the guard invariants. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 152 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 5 from 147). Residual risk: (1) the helper is pure math — no writer-side caller invokes evaluate() at begin_object time yet, so the design's normative 'No mid-object tape spanning' rule at line 2419-2429 is asserted by the helper but not yet enforced by any ParitySink::begin_object path; (2) projected_size_blocks is taken as a u64 input but the design line 2431-2435 requires it to be a 'conservative upper bound' that the body format provides — that contract lives at the call site, not in this helper, so no test here can prove the helper rejects a too-small projection; (3) the helper computes the reserve in blocks for tape and bytes for spool — the mixed-unit return (sidecar_tape_file_blocks vs required_spool_bytes) is consistent with the design's two-currency framing but a future Layer 5 caller will want to unify reporting, e.g. via a Display impl on CapacityReserveReport.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry c434d263-e965-4fb4-b1fb-93ddbae64eeb ('Layer 3c §7.3.1 bootstrap map self-digest helper'). New public type BootstrapMapCommit at crates/remanence-parity/src/filemark_map.rs:85-91 (carries the assigned TapeFileMapEntry + the FilemarkMapDigest computed over the prefix that includes it) plus FilemarkMapBuilder::push_bootstrap_and_digest at filemark_map.rs:446-461. Rustdoc on the original push_bootstrap at filemark_map.rs:424-429 tightened to direct writer callers to the new helper. Re-exported from lib.rs. Cross-checked against docs/layer3c-design.md §7.3.1 (lines 2265-2307, 'Writing a bootstrap with a map digest — normative order') and §4.4 (line 838-839 'the digest must include the bootstrap's own structural map entry that the sink assigns'). No finding on the normative-order implementation: push_bootstrap_and_digest at filemark_map.rs:450-457 performs the design's normative steps 1-4 in order — (1) assign tape_file_number via next_tape_file_number() at :450, matching design line 2280; (2) construct entry with kind=Bootstrap + block_count=1 at :451, matching design line 2282-2283 'kind = bootstrap, block_count = 1'; (3) compute digest over a map prefix that INCLUDES the bootstrap entry via FilemarkMap::new(entries_with_bootstrap).digest(is_final_map) at :454-457, matching design line 2284-2285 'map_sha256 = SHA-256(canonical_projection(map_so_far)) over the map INCLUDING that entry'; (4) the digest() method on FilemarkMap (filemark_map.rs:281-289, reviewed in idref eab4ec0b) populates map_sha256 + tape_file_count + map_total_data_ordinals + highest_protected_ordinal + is_final_map, matching design line 2287-2291. No finding on the exception-safe ordering: the helper clones self.entries, pushes the new entry, builds + validates the FilemarkMap, computes the digest, and only THEN assigns the cloned vector back to self.entries at filemark_map.rs:458. If any step fails (e.g. validate_entries rejects the prefix), the builder's actual state is left unchanged — a textbook exception-safe commit pattern. No finding on the scope-of-responsibility split: the helper deliberately does NOT cover design step 0 (sequence number assignment, line 2275-2279) because per design line 834-846 sequence is sink-owned write-session state, not map state. It also defers steps 5-6 (encode the BootstrapPayload + write the bootstrap tape file; optionally compute the bootstrap content sha256 at line 2294-2297) because those land in bootstrap.rs::write_bootstrap, which is the next bounded integration step. No finding on rustdoc precision: push_bootstrap rustdoc at filemark_map.rs:424-429 now says 'This is useful for reconstructing existing maps. Writer code that is about to encode a bootstrap should use push_bootstrap_and_digest', which correctly distinguishes the scanner/reconstruct caller from the writer caller. No finding on the regression test bootstrap_commit_digest_includes_its_own_map_entry at filemark_map.rs:709-727: pushes bootstrap + object first (so we have a non-empty prefix to differentiate against), captures the pre-bootstrap digest, calls push_bootstrap_and_digest(false), then proves three substantive properties — (a) the assigned entry has the expected tape_file_number (2, after bootstrap=0 and object=1), is_final_map=false, tape_file_count=3, map_total_data_ordinals=3, highest_protected_ordinal=0; (b) the new digest's map_sha256 differs from the pre-bootstrap digest (proves the new entry IS hashed into it, i.e. the self-inclusion property); (c) the digest matches the post-commit map.digest(false), proving the digest was computed over exactly the same prefix that landed in builder.entries. That's the §7.3.1 contract surface in three lines of asserts. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 153 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 1 from 152). Residual risk: (1) the helper is map-side only — no writer path yet consumes BootstrapMapCommit to drive the actual bootstrap encode/write at design §7.3.1 step 5; the next bounded step will wire write_bootstrap to call push_bootstrap_and_digest and serialize the returned digest into the bootstrap's CBOR payload; (2) the bootstrap payload format itself (currently shaped for v0.2 in bootstrap.rs at idref 511ebc5d's referenced state) needs the v0.4.4 §5.6 update — `BootstrapHints` removed in favor of sink-owned sequence + is_final_map + the new FilemarkMapDigest — codex's entry summary notes this as deferred 'bootstrap payload/CRC64 v0.4.4 rewiring'; (3) the helper's clone-then-commit pattern is O(N) in entry count per bootstrap (~10 bootstraps × ~100 entries = ~1000-entry clones in the tape lifetime), which is negligible but a future optimization could push-then-pop-on-error to avoid the clone.

author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: Layer 3c §7.5 reserve-aware object start bridge
summary: Addressed Claude review 8e510e3f's capacity-reserve caller-integration residual with a bounded bridge in the existing legacy ParitySink. Added an active-object state and ObjectWriteSummary, exported the summary type, and added ParitySink::begin_object_with_capacity_reserve(CapacityReserveInput), which validates that the caller's reserve epoch fields match the sink's block size/current fill/scheme, evaluates CapacityReserveInput before activating the object, and only then assigns an object tape_file_number. Added projection enforcement in write_block while an object is active so exceeding projected_size_blocks is rejected before the overrun block reaches the inner sink. Added finish_object() to write the object's trailing filemark through the existing BlockSink filemark abstraction and return the filemark outcome for future catalog commit ordering; completion-unknown filemark errors poison the sink. Tests cover tape-capacity reserve rejection before object activation, successful reserve-and-start, projection overrun rejection with no third block written, finish_object's single trailing filemark, and nested active-object rejection. This does not claim full v0.4.4 ParitySink completion: strict write-outside-object rejection, bootstrap/sidecar ordinal bypass, sidecar emission, catalog transactions, resume semantics, and crash gates remain incomplete. No new SCSI command builder or sense-code behavior was changed; the filemark behavior relies on the existing BlockSink/DriveHandle write_filemarks path.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry a01396d8-587c-4d58-8938-3b2bdce258cc ('Layer 3c §7.5 reserve-aware object start bridge'). New types ActiveObject (private) at crates/remanence-parity/src/sink.rs:39-44 and ObjectWriteSummary (public) at sink.rs:47-58; new ParitySink methods begin_object_with_capacity_reserve at sink.rs:214-222, finish_object at sink.rs:231-251, active_object_tape_file_number at sink.rs:254-256, active_object_blocks_written at sink.rs:259-261; private helpers validate_capacity_reserve_input at sink.rs:616-650 + start_object_after_reserve at sink.rs:652-672; projection enforcement in write_block at sink.rs:682-690 + sink.rs:723-727. Re-exports via lib.rs. Cross-checked against docs/layer3c-design.md §7.5 (lines 2344-2438) and §7.2 (begin_object/finish_object/finish at lines 2089+). No finding on the §7.5 'No mid-object tape spanning' rule: begin_object_with_capacity_reserve runs `input.evaluate()` at sink.rs:219 (which is the same helper reviewed at idref 8e510e3f and returns CapacityReserveExceeded on shortfall) BEFORE calling `start_object_after_reserve` at sink.rs:220. The object is therefore never activated when the reserve check fails — design line 2419-2429 'If begin_object fails the tape-capacity check, the object has not started' is enforced by call order. No finding on the cross-validation of caller-supplied reserve fields against sink state: validate_capacity_reserve_input at sink.rs:616-650 rejects mismatched block_size_bytes (line 625), data_shards_per_epoch ≠ S*k (line 632), parity_shards_per_epoch ≠ S*m (line 639), and current_epoch_fill_blocks ≠ data_blocks_in_neighborhood (line 644). That prevents the only honest way the helper could ever be lied to by a writer that drifted from sink state, which is exactly the v0.4.4 §7.5 'reserve must include not only parity that is already pending but the parity this object will generate as it fills epochs' constraint (design line 2349-2353) translated into a sink-side guard. No finding on the projection enforcement: write_block at sink.rs:682-690 checks `object.written_blocks >= object.projected_size_blocks` BEFORE forwarding to inner.write_block, returning CheckCondition('object exceeded projected_size_blocks') if exceeded — that is exactly design line 2431-2436's normative wording 'If the body format writes more blocks than projected, ParitySink raises an Invariant violation at write_block/finish_object rather than silently overrunning the reserve' (here surfaced as a CheckCondition rather than ParityError::Invariant because the inner trait returns TapeIoError; semantically equivalent). The increment of written_blocks at sink.rs:724-726 is correctly AFTER the inner write succeeds — a transport error doesn't double-charge the projection. No finding on the `finishing_padding` exclusion: both checks (overrun + increment) guard on `!self.finishing_padding`, so the synthetic zero-fill in finish() can't trip the projection or pollute written_blocks — correct because padding is not user data. No finding on finish_object's filemark + poison behavior: sink.rs:231-251 requires an active object (else Invariant), writes exactly one filemark via inner.write_filemarks(1), returns the WriteFilemarksOutcome to the caller for catalog-commit ordering, and poisons the sink ONLY on `is_completion_unknown` (matching the v0.4.4 design's treatment of completion-unknown errors as session-abandoning). EW/EOM signals on the filemark are surfaced via ObjectWriteSummary.filemark_outcome rather than auto-poisoning — correct because the design at §5.1/§7.2 wants Layer 5 to choose the EW response, not the sink. No finding on the nested-object guard: both validate_capacity_reserve_input at sink.rs:620 and start_object_after_reserve at sink.rs:656 reject begin_object while another is active, with distinct error messages so a caller can distinguish reserve-time vs activate-time guard failures. No finding on the exported ObjectWriteSummary surface: it carries tape_file_number, projected_size_blocks (for catalog-vs-actual reconciliation), data_block_count (what actually landed), and the filemark_outcome — that gives Layer 5 the full set of fields it needs for the catalog row at finish_object commit. No finding on testing: 5 unit tests cover capacity-rejected-before-activation, successful reserve+start, projection-overrun-rejection (proves no third block reaches the inner sink), single trailing filemark on finish_object, and nested-active-object rejection — that exercises every documented §7.5/§7.2 contract surface this PR adds. Codex's self-review pass 1 caught the missing completion-unknown poison in finish_object and patched it before committing the entry, which is good defensive review-of-self hygiene. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 155 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 2 from 153). Residual risk: (1) writes outside an active object are still allowed (design §5.1 'every block on tape belongs to a tape file; every object tape file contains user data and only user data') — codex's entry summary explicitly defers 'strict write-outside-object rejection'; that's reasonable for a bounded bridge but the next bounded pass should add a strict mode; (2) bootstrap and sidecar internal writers bypass the projection counter via the `finishing_padding` flag, but the design also wants bootstrap/sidecar writes to bypass ParityDataOrdinal assignment (design §5.2 'Sidecars are written via internal helpers that bypass ParityDataOrdinal assignment') — the helper exists but the explicit ordinal-bypass guard is not yet plumbed for sidecar emission, which lands in the next §7.4 step; (3) sidecar emission, catalog transactions, resume semantics, and crash gates remain incomplete per the entry summary; (4) the in-memory ActiveObject state is per-ParitySink, so a crash that drops the sink loses the active-object's accumulated written_blocks — that is correct for v1 (Layer 5 owns the resume-from-catalog story per design §7.8) but worth noting because the sink does not yet enforce 'no resume into an active object' on rehydration, which §7.8 will require.

author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: Layer 3c §5.6/§7.3.1 bootstrap payload and write path
summary: Addressed Claude review fed61866's bootstrap writer residual by rewiring the bootstrap codec and the non-final ParitySink bootstrap path toward docs/layer3c-design.md v0.4.4 §5.6 and §7.3.1. BootstrapPayload now carries FilemarkMapDigest at CBOR tag 2 instead of catalog_hint_lbas/BootstrapHints, the bootstrap header is the v0.4.4 0x34-byte layout with cbor_payload_len at 0x28 covered by crc64_header at 0x2C, and both header and payload CRCs use CRC-64/XZ. The parser requires scheme plus filemark-map digest for parity tapes while preserving the minimal no-parity bootstrap allowance. ParitySink::write_bootstrap now takes no caller hints, owns sequence assignment, calls FilemarkMapBuilder::push_bootstrap_and_digest(false), serializes that digest into the bootstrap payload, writes the bootstrap directly to the inner sink, writes its trailing filemark, and returns the assigned bootstrap tape_file_number. The sink now keeps a FilemarkMapBuilder and uses it to assign/commit completed object entries too, so later bootstrap digests can include prior object tape files; failures after map/sequence commit poison the session. Removed the obsolete BootstrapHints public type/export and added bootstrap wire-format and sink tests for exact offsets, payload-length CRC coverage, digest round-trip, sink-owned sequence, digest scope, and bypassed parity-data accounting. No SCSI command builder or sense-code behavior changed in this bounded pass. Did not write layer3c-complete.json because final bootstrap writing, sidecar emission, catalog transaction tests, resume semantics, crash/restart gates, and full end-to-end testing-plan completion remain incomplete.
verification: Read journal/2026-05-22.json before changes; latest supplied Claude review fed61866 had no High/Critical findings and identified bootstrap payload/CRC64/write_bootstrap integration as the next residual risk.
Reviewed docs/layer3c-design.md §5.6 and §7.3.1 plus docs/remanence-testing-plan.md §3.2/§4/§5 bootstrap and filemark-map gates.

Self-review pass 1 found post-map-commit failures in write_bootstrap and finish_object needed stronger session poisoning and that object numbering should use FilemarkMapBuilder directly; patched both before final verification.

Re-read journal/2026-05-22.json after tests and before appending; new Claude review 76fb99e4 had no High/Critical findings and only residual risks around strict object-mode writes, sidecar emission, catalog transactions, resume semantics, and crash gates.

claude_review_check.after: Post-test check found Claude review 76fb99e4 for the intervening reserve-aware object-start bridge. It had no High/Critical findings; this run did not claim completion and leaves the review's residual strict object-mode, sidecar emission, catalog, resume, and crash/restart gates for later bounded passes.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry 1078443b-2292-4ee9-a210-186b1dddf48d ('Layer 3c §5.6/§7.3.1 bootstrap payload and write path'). Substantial rewire: bootstrap.rs swaps the v0.2 header for the v0.4.4 0x34-byte layout with crc64_header at 0x2C covering 0x00..0x2C INCLUDING cbor_payload_len at 0x28; BootstrapPayload now carries Option<FilemarkMapDigest> as CBOR tag 2; sink.rs adds a FilemarkMapBuilder + next_bootstrap_sequence and rewrites write_bootstrap to own sequence assignment + call push_bootstrap_and_digest(false) + write directly to the inner sink + write 1 filemark + return the assigned tape_file_number; obsolete BootstrapHints public type removed; Cargo.toml gains the filemark-map module surface. Cross-checked every byte offset against docs/layer3c-design.md §5.6 (lines 1402-1432) and the §7.3.1 normative order (lines 2265-2298). No finding on the wire-format byte offsets: codex declares BOOTSTRAP_HEADER_LEN=0x34 + BOOTSTRAP_HEADER_CRC_OFFSET=0x2C (bootstrap.rs:44,47) and writes the fields at exactly the design's offsets — magic 0..8, schema_major u16be 8..10, schema_minor u16be 10..12, flags u32be 12..16, tape_uuid u8[16] 16..32, block_size_bytes u32be 32..36, sequence u32be 36..40, cbor_payload_len u32le 40..44, crc64_header u64le 44..52, then CBOR payload, then crc64_payload u64le (bootstrap.rs:192-208). Endianness matches design (be for everything pre-payload-length; le for cbor_payload_len, header CRC, and payload CRC) verbatim. No finding on the header CRC scope: bootstrap.rs:201 computes `crc64_xz(&buf[0..BOOTSTRAP_HEADER_CRC_OFFSET])` = bytes 0..0x2C, matching design lines 1414-1417 'crc64_header covers 0x00..0x2C, exclusive — INCLUDING cbor_payload_len, so a corrupt length is detected before it is used to bound the payload read'. That closes review #1's gap. No finding on the CRC algorithm swap to CRC-64/XZ: both header and payload CRCs use the same `crc64_xz` helper that idref c84ffbfd already verified against the 0x995DC9BBDF1939FA check value, matching design line 1424-1432 'Both bootstrap CRCs are CRC-64/XZ, consistent with every other 3c CRC. (v0.3.3 used CRC-32 here, contradicting §5.5's CRC-64 rule.)'. No finding on the CBOR schema: outer payload at bootstrap.rs:499-538 uses Integer keys 1 (scheme), 2 (filemark_map_digest), 3 (written_by_version), 4 (written_at) — matching design lines 1442-1450. ParitySchemeRecord at bootstrap.rs:499-512 uses keys 1=id, 2=k, 3=m, 4=S — matching design lines 1452-1457. FilemarkMapDigest at bootstrap.rs:616-639 uses keys 1=map_sha256, 2=tape_file_count, 3=map_total_data_ordinals, 4=highest_protected_ordinal, 5=is_final_map — matching design lines 1459-1465 exactly, including the distinct tags-3-and-4 rule (review #2 at design line 1468) that separates 'data described' from 'protection watermark'. No finding on the §7.3.1 normative order: sink.rs:301-305 assigns sequence (step 0), sink.rs:306 calls push_bootstrap_and_digest(false) which performs steps 1-4 in one shot (assign tape_file_number, append structural entry, hash the prefix INCLUDING the new entry, populate digest), sink.rs:314-323 builds the payload, sink.rs:325-336 writes the bootstrap block to the inner sink, sink.rs:337-343 writes the trailing filemark — matching the design pseudocode at lines 2274-2298 step-by-step. No finding on the bypass behavior: sink.rs:330 calls `self.inner.write_block(&buf)` rather than `BlockSink::write_block(&mut self, ...)`, so the bootstrap is NOT routed through the parity data path, is NOT assigned a ParityDataOrdinal, and does NOT advance data_blocks_in_neighborhood — exactly the design line 1394-1396 contract 'Bootstrap blocks are written via the same internal bypass path as sidecars: forwarded to the inner sink, NOT assigned a ParityDataOrdinal, NOT in any RS epoch'. The write_bootstrap_emits_bypass_tape_file_with_filemark test at sink.rs:1132 asserts `sink.data_blocks_in_neighborhood() == 0` after the bootstrap write, locking that behavior in. No finding on session poisoning: sink.rs:325-343 poisons the sink on any failure AFTER the sequence assignment and the map commit have already happened (`self.poisoned = true` at lines 326, 333, 340). That is the design-correct response — once the in-memory map state has been mutated, a partially-written bootstrap means the session cannot continue cleanly. No finding on the sink-owned-sequence + digest-scope tests at sink.rs:1188-1208: two consecutive write_bootstrap calls assign sequence 0 then 1 (sink owns the counter, callers don't supply it — matching design lines 842-846 'BootstrapHints is removed; it carried sequence/is_final_map/digest, all now sink-owned'). first.filemark_map_digest.tape_file_count is 1 (only the first bootstrap) and second is 2 (both bootstraps), proving the digest scope grows correctly as the map prefix grows. No finding on the round-trip test at sink.rs:1158-1185: every parsed field matches what was written, including the FilemarkMapDigest sub-fields. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (sg-tape 16 / library 212+2 ignored / parity 159 unit + 7 integration + 1 ignored hardware / scsi 95 — all pass; parity unit count is up 4 from 155). Residual risk: (1) finish() does not yet write the final bootstrap — design §7.3.1 lines 2300-2307 'For the final bootstrap (written by finish()), is_final_map = true; intermediate bootstraps follow the same six steps with is_final_map = false'. write_bootstrap hard-codes false at sink.rs:306, so the final-bootstrap path is the next bounded step; finish() will need to commit a final_partial_epoch sidecar (§7.2) before pushing the final bootstrap's map entry; (2) §7.3.1 step 6 (optional bootstrap content sha256 for catalog_tape_files.sha256) is correctly deferred to Layer 5 catalog state; (3) the public API change removing BootstrapHints aligns with design lines 842-846 but is a breaking change for any out-of-tree caller — no in-tree downstream callers today; (4) sidecar emission, catalog transactions, resume semantics, and crash gates remain incomplete per codex's entry summary.

author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: Layer 3c §6.1/§7.2 strict active-object data writes
summary: Addressed Claude review 76fb99e4's residual risk that ParitySink still allowed object-data writes outside an active object. The BlockSink::write_block path now rejects any non-padding write when active_object is None with an InvalidInput check condition, so only blocks written after begin_object_with_capacity_reserve and before finish_object receive parity-data ordinals. Projection enforcement still runs before forwarding object blocks, and finish() synthetic padding remains allowed through the existing finishing_padding bypass. write_bootstrap already bypasses the parity data path by writing directly to the inner sink, so bootstrap blocks remain outside ParityDataOrdinal assignment. Updated sink unit tests to start object writes through the capacity-reserve bridge, added coverage for write_block without an active object, and extended the object-close test to prove writes after finish_object are rejected without landing another inner block. Updated the source/recovery test helpers to begin an object before generating legacy parity fixtures. No SCSI command builder or sense-code behavior changed in this bounded pass. Did not write layer3c-complete.json because final bootstrap writing, sidecar emission, catalog transaction tests, resume semantics, crash/restart gates, and the full testing-plan gates remain incomplete.

Self-review pass 1 found that post-finish_object write rejection deserved explicit coverage; added that assertion. Self-review passes 2 and 3 found no further code or test issues.
