author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry 927e5503 (§7.5.1 multi-object EW interleaving success gate: sidecar_only_multi_object_early_warning_interleaves_without_state_leakage at sink.rs:2403). Finding [no finding]: this closes the multi-object EW residual I named in df0ff75c. The test interleaves two objects (5 blocks + 7 blocks = 12 = k*S) across a single epoch with EW signals deliberately placed at different points: object 1's trailing filemark has EW (sink.rs:2408 ew_filemarks=[1]), object 2's first and last data blocks (raw positions 6 and 12) have EW. The test pins critical state-isolation invariants: (a) object 1's EW filemark does NOT block the subsequent begin_object — second begin_object_with_capacity_reserve succeeds (sink.rs:2435-2440); (b) object 1's EW does NOT leak into object 2's outcomes — each block's outcome.early_warning is checked against the precise pattern (sink.rs:2446-2450 matches!(local, 1 | 7)); (c) the sidecar is NOT emitted after object 1 alone (first.sidecars_emitted.is_empty() at sink.rs:2432 — epoch only 5 blocks full, not yet k*S=12); (d) the sidecar IS emitted after object 2 completes the epoch (second.sidecars_emitted.len()=1 at sink.rs:2463); (e) the completed sidecar covers BOTH objects' ordinals (protected_ordinal_start=0, protected_ordinal_end_exclusive=12 at sink.rs:2466-2467); (f) sidecar's filemark is clean despite both objects' EW activity (sink.rs:2468-2469); (g) the final bootstrap digest's tape_file_count=4 (obj1/obj2/sidecar/bootstrap, sink.rs:2497) with map_total_data_ordinals=12 and highest_protected_ordinal=12. The capacity_input_with_current_fill(7, 10_000, block_size, 5) at sink.rs:2436-2438 explicitly pins that the second object starts with a partial epoch fill of 5 — a deliberate fixture choice that exercises the cross-object epoch boundary, not a fresh-epoch start. raw.ew_blocks_seen == [6, 12] (sink.rs:2478) is exact, catching either over-fire or under-fire of EW propagation in the multi-object setting. Combined with f488477c's single-object combined EW test, the §7.5.1 EW success-path matrix now covers {single-object intermittent EW, multi-object EW interleaving with cross-object epoch boundary, EW-on-filemark in either object} — the writer's EW state machine is exercised across the most realistic production patterns. Recommended fix: none. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (remanence-parity 330 passed, +1 from 329; library 214 passed; scsi 95 passed; integration suites 16/14/7 passed). Residual risk: the test crosses one object boundary inside a single epoch; a three-or-more-object session interleaving EW with multiple epoch closures is not separately exercised, but the cross-object state-isolation invariant is monotone. EW on the sidecar tape file's body blocks (during sidecar emission) is not directly exercised in this test — the sidecar is emitted only at the end and is clean here — but 8af69e1b covers sidecar-EW. EW combined with EOM in a multi-object session (e.g. EW on object 1, EOM on object 2's data) is not exercised; the existing single-object EW-then-EOM coverage from 1a173435 / 08afd5b3 generalizes.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry 0714e160 (§7.8 resume sidecar EW commit gate: emit_resume_rebuilt_sidecars_commits_despite_early_warning at resume.rs:1380, with fixture extensions RecordingResumeRawSink::with_early_warning_on_block at resume.rs:892 and with_early_warning_on_filemark at resume.rs:902). Finding [no finding]: this is the first resume-side EW coverage in the recent stream — extends §7.5.1's EW policy (already pinned for ordinary sink writes) to the §7.8 restart/append-after-crash path. Codex's entry has idref=null because this isn't closing a specific Claude residual but adding a fresh §7.8 coverage gap; the choice is appropriate given the EW matrix saturation on the regular sink side. The test builds a resume rebuild plan via rebuild_open_epoch_from_committed_prefix, then drives emit_resume_rebuilt_sidecars_to_raw with a RecordingResumeRawSink configured to emit EW on block 0 of the resume sidecar AND on its trailing filemark. It asserts: (a) raw_sink.ew_blocks_seen == [0] and ew_filemarks_seen == 1 (resume.rs:1411-1412) — fixture self-verifies both EW triggers fired; (b) result.sidecars_emitted[0].tape_file_number == 4 and its filemark_outcome.early_warning == true, end_of_medium == false (resume.rs:1413-1417) — the emitted SidecarTapeFile correctly propagates EW upstream without poisoning; (c) the catalog commit callback ran at append_position.lba + sidecar_blocks + 1 (resume.rs:1419-1427) — proving the commit happens *after* the EW-flagged synchronous filemark barrier, not after just the body block. The last assertion is the critical §7.8 invariant: resume-generated sidecars commit like ordinary sidecars, observing the §7.7 filemark durability barrier even when EW is asserted. The events.borrow().last() == Commit(4, lba+blocks+1) check would loudly fail if a regression made the commit fire on the wrong barrier (e.g. before the filemark, or before the EW filemark completes synchronously). The fixture extensions at resume.rs:892, 902 are bounded — only resume tests use the RecordingResumeRawSink — and don't disturb existing EOM behavior. Recommended fix: none. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (remanence-parity 333 passed, +1 from 332; library 214 passed; scsi 95 passed; integration suites 16/14/7 passed). Residual risk: only block 0 of the resume sidecar is EW-flagged; an intermittent EW pattern across multiple body blocks of a longer resume sidecar (the same kind of pattern f488477c covered for non-resume sidecars) is not separately exercised. author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry e39017cf (§7.8 multi-sidecar resume EW/EOM gate: emit_resume_rebuilt_sidecars_rejects_second_sidecar_filemark_eom_after_first_commit at resume.rs:1561, with indexed filemark helpers with_eom_on_filemark_index at resume.rs:904 and with_early_warning_on_filemark_index at resume.rs:914). The fixture uses a map with 8 ordinals that rebuilds into 2 sidecars (resume.rs:1576 asserts rebuild.rebuilt_sidecars.len() == 2). EW is placed on the first body block of the *second* sidecar (resume.rs:1586), and EW+EOM on the second sidecar's filemark (resume.rs:1587-1588). The error assertion at resume.rs:1606-1619 is unusually strong: it requires the message to name the *second* tape file number ('resume sidecar {N+1}'), prove it's the filemark phase ('synchronous filemark reached end-of-medium'), and pin the commit prohibition ('before catalog commit'). This ensures the error correctly identifies which sidecar in the multi-sidecar sequence failed. The expected event stream at resume.rs:1623-1640 is the strongest part: it pins a precise 8-event sequence — [Position(append_lba), N×WriteBlock(first sidecar), WriteFilemark, Position(first_barrier_lba), Commit(first sidecar at barrier), Position(first_barrier_lba) for inter-sidecar check, M×WriteBlock(second sidecar), WriteFilemark]. The absence of any post-barrier Position event for the second sidecar and the absence of a second Commit event proves the abort is immediate. The presence of the first commit *before* the second sidecar's writes proves the per-sidecar commit barrier is independent: a successful first commit doesn't carry forward to make the second commit acceptable when its filemark fails. The fixture helper extensions at resume.rs:904, 914 (with_*_on_filemark_index) preserve backward compatibility with the existing first-filemark default helpers used by 5b3d809a and 9d289ac4. Recommended fix: none. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (remanence-parity 339 passed, +1 from 338; library 214 passed; scsi 95 passed; integration suites 16/14/7 passed). A three-or-more sidecar resume case (where the *third* sidecar fails after two clean commits) is not exercised, but the per-sidecar independence is monotone in the loop iteration. The case where the first sidecar commit callback itself returns Err (catalog failure) and EW/EOM lands on the second sidecar isn't exercised — the callback Err path is handled in other resume tests but not combined with EW/EOM here.

EW is on the second sidecar's first body block (block index = first_sidecar_blocks), EOM is on the second body block (block index = first_sidecar_blocks + 1). The ResumeAppend error at resume.rs:1693-1706 names 'resume sidecar {N+1}', pins 'block 1 reached end-of-medium' (the *per-sidecar* block index, not the global), and forbids commit. Combined with 9d289ac4 (1st sidecar body+filemark EOM after EW), 5b3d809a (success), and e39017cf (2nd sidecar filemark EOM after EW), the resume EW/EOM matrix is now fully exercised for 2-sidecar topology. The test asserts (a) finish_object on the first object commits cleanly with sidecar.filemark_outcome.early_warning==true (sink.rs:2572-2573); (b) begin_object_with_capacity_reserve + write_block for the second object's first data block both succeed (sink.rs:2577-2587) — confirming §7.5.1 EW propagates as advisory only; (c) finish_object on the second object returns ParityError::Invariant with 'object trailing filemark reached end of medium' (sink.rs:2589-2600) — directly exercising the production gate at sink.rs:786-790, which sets self.poisoned=true and returns Invariant *before* push_object updates the filemark map; (d) filemark_map.entries() contains exactly object(0,12,0) and parity_sidecar(1, sidecar_block_count, 0,0,12) (sink.rs:2607-2614) — no second-object entry was pushed; (e) finish() returns Invariant('poisoned') (sink.rs:2616-2624) so the poisoned session refuses the final bootstrap. The post-block diagnostics at sink.rs:2627-2633 are tight: ew_filemarks_seen==[2] (sidecar trailing), eom_filemarks_seen==[3] (object trailing), block_count == 12 + sidecar_block_count + 1 (exactly one post-sidecar block). author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry 4e6b4af2 (§7.5.1 sidecar trailing filemark EW followed by later sidecar-body EOM gate: sidecar_filemark_early_warning_does_not_mask_later_sidecar_body_eom at sink.rs:2651). Finding [no finding]: this closes the second-object-sidecar-body-EOM residual I named in 466559d2. The fixture is sharp: raw sink fires EW only on filemark_count==2 (sink.rs:2681, the FIRST sidecar's trailing filemark) and fires EOM only on the first WriteBlock after filemark_count >= 3 (sink.rs:2666, gated by 'filemark_count >= 3 && eom_blocks_seen.is_empty()'). This pins the EOM to the FIRST block of the SECOND object's sidecar body — landing AFTER the second object's data + trailing filemark are clean. The test asserts (a) first finish_object commits with first_sidecar.filemark_outcome.early_warning==true (sink.rs:2727); (b) 12 second-object data blocks write clean (sink.rs:2738-2744); (c) second finish_object returns ParityError::Invariant with 'sidecar block write reached end of medium' (sink.rs:2746-2757) — directly exercising the production gate at sink.rs:1481-1486 which sets self.poisoned=true and returns Invariant BEFORE the second sidecar's trailing filemark is written; (d) crucially, the filemark map at sink.rs:2764-2772 now contains THREE entries: object(0,12,0) + parity_sidecar(1, first_sidecar_block_count, 0,0,12) + object(2,12,12) — the second OBJECT entry is committed (its filemark wrote clean) but no second sidecar entry exists. This is precisely §7.5.1's data-committed semantics applied to sidecar-body EOM: the data and object filemark that already committed remain in the catalog, but the sidecar that failed to complete does not. (e) Poisoned-retry: second finish_object call (sink.rs:2774-2782) and final finish (sink.rs:2784-2792) both return Invariant('poisoned'), proving no retry-after-poison is allowed. The diagnostics at sink.rs:2795-2802 are tight: ew_filemarks_seen==[2] (only first sidecar trailing), filemark_count==3 (object1-filemark + sidecar1-filemark + object2-filemark, NOT including any second sidecar filemark), eom_blocks_seen[0] == 12 + first_sidecar_block_count + 12 + 1 (precisely the first block of the second sidecar body, landing AFTER 12 first-object data + first sidecar body + 12 second-object data). The expected_events stream at sink.rs:2804-2817 pins exactly 12 + 1 + first_sidecar_block_count + 1 + 12 + 1 + 1 = the canonical sequence ending with the EOM-tripped second sidecar's first body block — with NO subsequent WriteFilemark (so no second sidecar trailing filemark) and NO bootstrap blocks. Critically, this test exposes that §7.5.1's commit semantics asymmetrically protect already-committed objects: the second object IS in the filemark map without its sidecar, which is a documented unprotected-data state under §5.4/§9.2 (the recovery path will surface the second object as unprotected). The production gate ordering at sink.rs:1481 (EOM-detect inside the sidecar body block loop, BEFORE the trailing filemark) is the load-bearing invariant — if the gate were moved AFTER the trailing filemark, a 4th raw filemark would appear and the second sidecar would be partially committed. Combined with prior §7.5.1 coverage (e43a2042 object-data EOM, 352b3c0c object-filemark EOM, a46cb6f0 sidecar-body EW + 84b1bf79 sidecar-filemark EW), the §7.5.1 cross-tape-file EW-then-EOM matrix at the 2-tape-file topology now spans EW on {sidecar body, sidecar trailing filemark} × subsequent EOM on {next object's first data block, next object's trailing filemark, next object's sidecar body first block}. Recommended fix: none. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (remanence-parity 346 passed, +1 from 345; library 214 passed; scsi 95 passed; integration suites 16/14/7 passed). Residual risk: sidecar-trailing-filemark EW followed by EOM on the second object's *sidecar trailing filemark* (not sidecar body) is not separately exercised — this would test the production gate at sink.rs:1489+ (sidecar trailing filemark write) on the second-tape-file path. EOM on a *later* (non-first) block of the second sidecar body is not covered (the small_scheme()/12-block-object combination may produce a single-block second sidecar, so the 'later block' case may not be reachable without scheme changes). Three-tape-file chains (sidecar-EW propagating across two subsequent objects with EOM only on the third object's sidecar body) are not exercised. The unprotected-object map state (object(2,12,12) without its sidecar) is asserted at sink.rs:2764-2772 but no downstream test exercises the recovery-side handling of this exact catalog state — that's the next-recovery-test residual to watch.

author: claude

summary: Review of codex entry 0ea10ddc (§7.5.1 sidecar trailing filemark EW followed by later sidecar-trailing-filemark EOM gate: sidecar_filemark_early_warning_does_not_mask_later_sidecar_filemark_eom at sink.rs:2821). The fixture is sharp: raw sink hard-codes early_warning=false/end_of_medium=false on every write_fixed_block (sink.rs:2839-2840), fires EW only on filemark_count==2 (sink.rs:2847, first sidecar trailing filemark), and fires EOM only on filemark_count==4 (sink.rs:2851, second sidecar trailing filemark). This pins EOM to the SECOND sidecar's trailing filemark — landing AFTER the second object's data, the second object's trailing filemark, AND the entire second sidecar's body have all written clean. The test asserts (a) first finish_object commits with first_sidecar.filemark_outcome.early_warning==true (sink.rs:2897); (b) second object data + finish_object proceed normally until the sidecar trailing filemark; (c) finish_object on the second object returns ParityError::Invariant with 'sidecar trailing filemark reached end of medium' (sink.rs:2916-2927) — directly exercising the production gate at sink.rs:1496-1501 which sets self.poisoned=true and returns Invariant BEFORE push_parity_sidecar updates the filemark map; (d) filemark map at sink.rs:2934-2942 contains object(0,12,0) + parity_sidecar(1, first_sidecar_block_count, 0,0,12) + object(2,12,12) — the second object IS committed (its trailing filemark wrote clean as filemark 3) but the second sidecar is NOT (its trailing filemark was filemark 4, the EOM one); (e) poisoned retry — finish_object (sink.rs:2944-2952) and finish (sink.rs:2954-2962) both return Invariant('poisoned'). The diagnostics at sink.rs:2965-2973 are crisp: second_sidecar_body_blocks == first_sidecar_block_count (the second sidecar body completed in full before the filemark EOM tripped — matching small_scheme()/12-block-object geometry), ew_filemarks_seen==[2], eom_filemarks_seen==[4], filemark_count==4 (object0 + sidecar0 + object2 + sidecar2's EOM trailing filemark — NO fifth filemark for any rewrite/bootstrap). The expected_events stream at sink.rs:2975-2992 pins the canonical 4-filemark sequence: 12 data + WriteFilemark + N sidecar1 body + WriteFilemark(EW) + 12 data + WriteFilemark + N sidecar2 body + WriteFilemark(EOM) — and nothing after. The catalog ends in the same 'object-without-sidecar' unprotected state I flagged in 4012ffdc, but the divergence between 4e6b4af2 (EOM on sidecar-body first block) and this test (EOM on sidecar-trailing filemark, AFTER full body) cleanly separates two distinct §7.5.1 commit-barrier points: the body-block gate at sink.rs:1481-1486 versus the filemark gate at sink.rs:1496-1501. Both leave the same catalog state but exercise different production code paths. author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry cf24d3e0 (§9.2 recovery-side handling of object-without-sidecar catalog state: object_committed_without_sidecar_is_pending_epoch_before_tape_io at recovery.rs:1371). Finding [no finding]: this closes the recovery-side cc3b4c16 residual I named — the very-next reviewer task after the §7.5.1 EW-then-EOM matrix saturation. The fixture builds the exact catalog state produced by 4e6b4af2/0ea10ddc (sidecar-body or sidecar-filemark EOM after the second object's data and trailing filemark committed): bootstrap(0,1) + object(1, watermark=4, 0) + parity_sidecar(2, ..., 0,0,4) + object(3, 2, 4) at recovery.rs:1377-1382, with the second object's ordinals [4,6) AFTER the protection watermark. The test asserts three load-bearing invariants: (a) catalog addressability — position_for_ordinal(watermark + 1 = 5) maps to (tape_file=3, block_within_file=1) at recovery.rs:1385-1391, proving the unprotected second object IS still catalog-locatable; this means a reader can ask 'where is ordinal 5?' and get an answer — the data exists on tape and is not lost; (b) recovery gate — recover_object_block_from_sidecar(tape_file=3, body_lba=1) returns ParityError::UnrecoverablePendingEpoch { failed_ordinal: 5, watermark: 4 } at recovery.rs:1394-1405, directly exercising the production gate at filemark_map.rs:649-655 ('if ordinal >= watermark, return UnrecoverablePendingEpoch'); (c) no-tape-I/O early-bail — raw.configured_block_size == None and raw.position() == PhysicalPositionHint::new(0) at recovery.rs:1406-1407, proving the gate fires at recovery.rs:103 (scoped_map.scope.recoverable(failed_ordinal)?) BEFORE the source.configure_fixed_block_size(block_size)? at recovery.rs:104. The ordering is critical: if the watermark check were after configure_block_size or position, an unrecoverable-pending-epoch call would still poke the SCSI device with a Mode Select. The test's raw.configured_block_size==None invariant guards that ordering. The UnrecoverablePendingEpoch error name carries the right semantic: 'not corrupt, not lost, just not-yet-protected — wait for a resume sidecar to commit through the ordinary §7.8 path before retrying recovery'. This is precisely the design intent in §7.5.1's data-committed semantics and §9.2's recoverability invariants: an object's data + trailing filemark may commit before its sidecar, leaving it unprotected; recovery must surface this as a pending state, not as data loss. Combined with 4e6b4af2/0ea10ddc's producer-side coverage and this consumer-side test, the round-trip 'EOM during sidecar emission → catalog state → recovery refuses pre-tape-I/O' is now fully closed at the §9.2 + §7.5.1 interface. Recommended fix: none. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (remanence-parity 348 passed, +1 from 347; library 214 passed; scsi 95 passed; integration suites 16/14/7 passed). Residual risk: the test only exercises the second object's first body block (body_lba=1); recovery of body_lba=0 (which is the first block of tape_file=3 but technically a filemark in some catalog encodings — let me verify) or body_lba=second_object_blocks-1 (last block) is not separately tested, though the gate is body_lba-independent so this is low value. The scan-side equivalent (scan.rs UnrecoverablePendingEpoch at scan.rs:953) is not separately tested in this PR — if scan classification ever processes an object-without-sidecar catalog, it may need its own no-tape-I/O assertion. The 'recover after a resume sidecar commits' round-trip (object-without-sidecar → resume rebuild emits sidecar → recovery now succeeds) is not exercised end-to-end; producing such a test would close the §7.8 + §9.2 loop. The error variant carries failed_ordinal and watermark but no tape_file_number context — if downstream pretty-printing wants to surface 'tape file 3 is unprotected', that's caller-side enrichment, not a gate change.

summary: Review of codex entry f88eda32 (§7.5.1 + §7.8 + §9.2 end-to-end resume-sidecar-then-recovery round-trip: resume_committed_sidecar_makes_object_without_sidecar_recoverable at recovery.rs:1498). The fixture is the gold-standard four-stage end-to-end: (Stage 1, pre-resume, recovery.rs:1511-1554) builds the cf24d3e0-style unprotected catalog (bootstrap + object + sidecar + later object) where the second object's data is committed without sidecar protection, then asserts recover_object_block_from_sidecar at tape_file=3/body_lba=1 returns ParityError::UnrecoverablePendingEpoch{failed_ordinal: 5, watermark: 4} with pending_raw.configured_block_size==None and pending_raw.position()==0 — re-confirming the no-tape-I/O pre-gate semantics from cf24d3e0. (Stage 2, resume rebuild, recovery.rs:1556-1580) runs rebuild_open_epoch_from_committed_prefix against the same committed records, asserting rebuild.plan.append_after_tape_file_number==3 (resume sidecar lands after the unprotected object), highest_protected_ordinal_before_rebuild==4 + highest_protected_ordinal_after_rebuild==8 (watermark advancement), rebuilt_sidecars.len()==1 (one resume sidecar covers the open epoch), and live_epoch.is_none() (the second object is a full rebuildable epoch, no live continuation needed). The carefully-chosen scheme(2,1,2) + 4-block second object gives second_object_end - first_watermark == data_shards_per_epoch (asserted at recovery.rs:1506-1510) — pinning the fixture to a 'one full rebuildable epoch' geometry. (Stage 3, resume sidecar commits via ordinary barrier path, recovery.rs:1582-1617) calls emit_resume_rebuilt_sidecars_to_raw with a commit-callback that records (tape_file_number, position_after.lba). Asserts: sidecars_emitted.len()==1, tape_file_number==4 (resume sidecar appended after the last committed tape file), epoch_id==1, protected_ordinal_start==4 / end_exclusive==8, highest_protected_ordinal==8 (advanced from 4), commit_events == [(4, position_after)] — proving the resume sidecar commits through the SAME §7.7 commit-durability-barrier path as any ordinary sidecar (the catalog commit callback fires AFTER the filemark+Position barrier, not as part of the body emission). (Stage 4, post-resume recovery succeeds, recovery.rs:1619-1677) extends the committed map with the resume sidecar entry, marks the same body_lba as unreadable, and asserts recover_object_block_from_sidecar now SUCCEEDS: recovered.failed_ordinal==5 (same ordinal as Stage 1's UnrecoverablePendingEpoch), recovered.recovered_block == second_object_blocks[failed_body_lba] (byte-equal to the original), recovered.sidecar_tape_file_number==4 (the resume-generated sidecar, NOT the original first sidecar), recovered.lost_shards == [Data{index: 0}] (correctly identifies the damaged shard position), and crucially recovery_raw.configured_block_size == Some(BLOCK_SIZE) — proving tape I/O DID happen on the success path (contrast Stage 1 where configure_block_size stayed None). This last invariant is the symmetric counterpart of cf24d3e0's no-tape-I/O assertion and seals the contrast: pending = no I/O, recoverable = I/O performed. The same source bytes (second_object_blocks[1] = block(12)) flow round-trip from producer through unprotected-catalog state through resume reconstruction through recovery damage simulation back to the recovered block, verified by direct array equality at recovery.rs:1664-1667. Recommended fix: none. This is the cleanest end-to-end test in the §7.5.1/§7.8/§9.2 trifecta. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (remanence-parity 350 passed, +1 from 349; library 214 passed; scsi 95 passed; integration suites 16/14/7 passed). Residual risk: the test only exercises a *full-epoch* second object — the live-epoch case (partial-epoch second object where rebuild.live_epoch.is_some()) is not covered by this round-trip, though prior coverage at 5b3d809a/9d289ac4/cfc66851/e39017cf/2ae7052a/cd12bd44 exercises live-epoch resume paths individually. Multi-tape-file unprotected runs (object → sidecar → object1 → object2 with both later objects unprotected, then resume rebuilds two sidecars at once) are not exercised as a round-trip. Damage on the resume sidecar's own body or filemark during emission is not exercised in this round-trip (the EW/EOM during resume cases are covered in 9d289ac4/cfc66851/etc but not combined with subsequent recovery). The test asserts the SAME ordinal+body_lba is recoverable; recovery of a different ordinal in the same epoch (e.g. body_lba=0 or body_lba=3) is not separately exercised, though §9.2's per-stripe symmetry should make the lba-choice low-value.

author: chatgpt
subject: implementation: Layer 3c section 7.5.1 sidecar-body EW symmetric EOM matrix
summary: Addressed Claude review f61a5591's remaining low-value but explicit symmetric EW/EOM matrix residuals for first-sidecar body early warning. Added a reusable SidecarBodyEwThenLaterEomRawTapeSink fixture and three tests in sink.rs: sidecar_body_early_warning_does_not_mask_later_object_data_eom, sidecar_body_early_warning_does_not_mask_later_object_filemark_eom, and sidecar_body_early_warning_does_not_mask_later_sidecar_body_eom. Together with the existing sidecar_body_early_warning_does_not_mask_later_sidecar_filemark_eom test, the body-EW row now covers later EOM on object data, object trailing filemark, sidecar body, and sidecar trailing filemark. The tests assert that object-data and object-filemark EOM leave only the first object plus first sidecar in the filemark map, while sidecar-body EOM leaves the later object committed without its sidecar, matching the section 7.5.1 commit barriers. They also assert the poisoned retry/final-finish gate and exact raw write/filemark event sequences. This was a test-only Layer 3c change; no production logic, public API, SCSI command, sense-code classification, rem-tar body format, filemark-map format, catalog implementation, or crash/restart production behavior changed. Did not write layer3c-complete.json because the full v0.4.4 surface and end-to-end testing-plan gates, including external catalog and live/QuadStor gates, remain incomplete.

author: claude
subject: review
summary: Review of codex entry c5d8f5f1 (§7.5.1 sidecar-body EW × {ObjectData, ObjectFilemark, SidecarBody} EOM symmetric matrix: three tests at sink.rs:3107, sink.rs:3202, sink.rs:3303 plus shared SidecarBodyEwThenLaterEomRawTapeSink fixture at sink.rs:3003). Finding [no finding]: this closes the three remaining sidecar-body-EW residual cells I named in f61a5591. The fixture design is clean — a single parameterized raw sink (sink.rs:2995-3077) with a SidecarBodyEwLaterEomTarget enum (ObjectData/ObjectFilemark/SidecarBody) that drives EOM landing per match arm in write_fixed_block (sink.rs:3037-3045) and write_filemark (sink.rs:3060-3061). The shared commit_first_full_object_with_sidecar_body_ew helper (sink.rs:3079-3104) factors out the common first-object setup: writes 12 data blocks, finish_object, asserts first_sidecar.filemark_outcome.early_warning==FALSE (the EW landed on the body, not the filemark) — the same body-vs-filemark distinction I verified in 9045b889. All three tests pin EW to raw block_count==13 (first sidecar body's first block) via `filemark_count==1 && ew_blocks_seen.is_empty()` at sink.rs:3033 — same condition as 9045b889. Test 1 (ObjectData EOM at sink.rs:3107): EOM lands on the second object's first data block (filemark_count>=2 trigger). Production gate at sink.rs:1650-1655 fires with 'object data block write reached end of medium' wrapped in TapeIoError::CheckCondition(ScsiError::InvalidInput) at sink.rs:3129-3137. Filemark map contains only object(0,12,0) + parity_sidecar(1, first_sidecar_block_count, 0,0,12) (sink.rs:3144-3151). eom_blocks_seen == [12 + first_sidecar_block_count + 1] (sink.rs:3179-3183) — precisely the second object's first block. filemark_count==2 (no second object filemark). Test 2 (ObjectFilemark EOM at sink.rs:3202): one clean second-object data block, then finish_object triggers WriteFilemark with EOM (filemark_count==3 trigger). Production gate at sink.rs:786-790 fires with ParityError::Invariant 'object trailing filemark reached end of medium' (sink.rs:3229-3237). Filemark map contains only object(0,12,0) + parity_sidecar(1,...) — no second object entry, because the gate fires BEFORE push_object. eom_filemarks_seen==[3], block_count == 12 + first_sidecar_block_count + 1 (sink.rs:3279-3285). Test 3 (SidecarBody EOM at sink.rs:3303): 12 clean second-object data blocks, then finish_object writes object filemark (3, clean), then sidecar body — first block hits EOM (filemark_count>=3 trigger). Production gate at sink.rs:1481-1486 fires with ParityError::Invariant 'sidecar block write reached end of medium' (sink.rs:3329-3340). Filemark map contains object(0,12,0) + parity_sidecar(1,...) + object(2,12,12) — second object IS committed (its filemark wrote clean as filemark 3), but no second sidecar. eom_blocks_seen == [12 + first_sidecar_block_count + 12 + 1] (sink.rs:3384-3388) — first block of the second sidecar body. All three tests assert poisoned-retry on finish_object and finish, plus pin the exact raw event stream. The matrix is now fully saturated: EW ∈ {sidecar body, sidecar trailing filemark} × EOM ∈ {object data first block, object trailing filemark, sidecar body first block, sidecar trailing filemark} = 8 cells covered (4e6b4af2, 9045b889, 0ea10ddc, e43a2042, 352b3c0c, c5d8f5f1's three tests, plus the prior 9045b889 sidecar-filemark EOM). The crisp body-vs-filemark distinction at sink.rs:3097-3100 — first_sidecar.filemark_outcome.early_warning is asserted FALSE in every fixture using this helper — is the load-bearing semantic distinguishing this matrix row from the EW-on-filemark row, and it's enforced by the helper. Recommended fix: none. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check (clean), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean), cargo test --workspace (remanence-parity 353 passed, +3 from 350; library 214 passed; scsi 95 passed; integration suites 16/14/7 passed). Residual risk: the body-EW location is hard-coded to the first sidecar's first body block in all four body-EW tests (block 13 = 12 data + 1 object filemark + 1). EW on a *later* block of the first sidecar body (e.g. block 2 or 3) followed by the same EOM landings is not separately exercised, but the production gate is positional-independent so this is low-value. Three-tape-file chains with body-EW on the first sidecar and EOM landing on the third tape-file's components are still not exercised. The non-first-block second sidecar body EOM cell (sidecar-body EW × EOM on the second sidecar's non-first block, with second sidecar large enough to have multiple blocks) is also not exercised, but small_scheme()/12-block-object geometry may not produce multi-block second sidecars. Symmetric EW-on-filemark × EOM-on-{various second sidecar non-first block positions} is also low-value but uncovered.
